texto: People who have cut their teeth on philosophical problems of rationality, knowledge, perception, free will and other minds are well placed to think better about problems of evidence, decision making, responsibility and ethics that life throws up.
explicacao: Citação de Simon Blackburn sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “People who have cut their teeth on philosophical problems of rationality, knowledge, perception, …” articula reflexao, epistemology, evidence em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como evidence e free-will.

texto: It is the thought that the least efficient way of of finding either happiness or pleasure is to pursue them. Put in terms of happiness, we can see it like this: To be happy you must quite literally "lose yourself". You must lose yourself in some pursuit; you need to forget your own happiness and find other goals and projects, other objects of concern that might include the welfare of some other people, or the cure of the disease, or simply in the variety of everyday activities with their little successes and setbacks.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Simon Blackburn: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “It is the thought that the least efficient way of of finding either happiness or pleasure is to p…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que felicidade, happiness, inspiracional pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e inspiracional.

frase_original: It is the thought that the least efficient way of of finding either happiness or pleasure is to pursue them. Put in terms of happiness, we can see it like this: To be happy you must quite literally "lose yourself". You must lose yourself in some pursuit; you need to forget your own happiness and find other goals and projects, other objects of concern that might include the welfare of some other people, or the cure of the disease, or simply in the variety of everyday activities with their little successes and setbacks.

texto: There are normal times when it is wholly admirable to be steadfast, resolute, unconflicted, and therefore when integrity is unmistakenly a virtue. The person of integrity knows what to do, and does it. But as we have been exploring, there are also times when certainty and single-mindedness indicate something less admirable: a deafness to voices that should be heard or a blindness to aspects of a situation that need to be considered.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Simon Blackburn sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “There are normal times when it is wholly admirable to be steadfast, resolute, unconflicted, and t…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, ethics, integrity, útil em momentos de integrity e person.

The person of integrity knows what to do, and does it. But as we have been exploring, there are also times when certainty and single-mindedness indicate something less admirable: a deafness to voices that should be heard or a blindness to aspects of a situation that need to be considered.
